expectations.evidence: BW Boek 7, Art. 7:290–310 — Middenstandsbedrijfsruimte (commercial retail/hospitality rental law)... The threshold question is whether your property qualifies as middenstandsbedrijfsruimte under Art. 7:290 BW. ... You have confirmed this is a 7:290 property, so this regime — with its relatively strong tenant protections — applies. ... The 7:290 regime gives tenants significantly more protection than, for example, Art. 7:230a commercial space.

Step 1: Ingebrekestelling (Notice of Default) — Art. 6:82 BW. Before you can claim ontbinding, you must formally put the tenant in default (ingebrekestelling) by sending a written demand... Under Art. 6:265 BW, every failure in the performance of an obligation entitles the other party to dissolve the agreement (ontbinden)... Three months of unpaid rent is a serious breach (tekortkoming in de nakoming) that Dutch courts regularly accept as justifying ontbinding of a commercial lease and the accompanying ontruiming (eviction).

The kantonrechter (part of the Rechtbank) has jurisdiction over commercial lease disputes, including 7:290 properties, under Art. 93–94 Rv (competentie kantonrechter in huurzaken). ... The kantonrechter will: 1. Schedule a hearing (zitting) 2. Allow the tenant to file a defense... 4. Issue a vonnis (judgment): Granting ontbinding and ontruiming (most likely outcome for 3 months non-payment without valid defense).
This is an AI-generated legal analysis and does NOT constitute legal advice within the meaning of the Dutch Advocates Act (Advocatenwet).
